_comment: Average private health insurance premiums — FY 2025-26 estimates based on APRA and industry data
_note: Premium figures are industry averages and vary significantly by insurer, state, excess, and inclusions. Used for calculator estimates only — users should check real policy prices on privatehealth.gov.au.
source: APRA Quarterly Private Health Insurance Statistics / privatehealth.gov.au

hospitalPremiums._comment: Average annual hospital-only premiums by tier for a single adult, no LHC loading, before rebate. Based on industry averages as at April 2026.

gold.label: Gold
gold.description: Covers all hospital services including cardiac, pregnancy, joint replacements, psychiatric, palliative care, and rehabilitation

silver.label: Silver
silver.description: Covers most hospital services — excludes some elective procedures like weight loss surgery, assisted reproduction, and some cosmetic

bronze.label: Bronze
bronze.description: Core hospital cover with restricted access to many services — limited to rehabilitation, psychiatric, and palliative care beyond the minimum

basic.label: Basic
basic.description: Very limited cover — rehabilitation, psychiatric, and palliative care only. Essentially a tax product to avoid MLS

familyMultipliers._comment: Multiply single premium by these factors for other policy types. Industry average ratios.

excessOptions._comment: Common excess (front-end deductible) levels. Higher excess = lower premium.

extrasPremiums._comment: Average annual extras (general treatment) premiums for a single adult. Extras cover is separate from hospital cover.

combinedAverages._comment: Average combined (hospital + extras) premium across all tiers and cover types, from APRA data

premiumGrowth._comment: Historical premium increase rates (applied each April)
premiumGrowth.current2026Description: 4.41% average increase from 1 April 2026 — highest since 2017. Range across insurers: 1.98% to 5.98%.
premiumGrowth.projectionDefaultDescription: Calculator default growth rate for 10-year projections — based on 2026 increase rate
